title: U.S. Steel Tariffs on Chinese Steel Exports
section_path: U.S. Steel Tariffs on Chinese Steel Exports
section_path_text: U.S. Steel Tariffs on Chinese Steel Exports
summary:
Main
Dry Weekly Market Monitor - Week 10, 2025 Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions March 05, 2025
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-03-06 133350.png | assets/2025-03-06_us-steel-tariffs-on-chinese-steel-exports_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_d3921b91598a.png
The recent reinstatement of a 25% tariff on steel imports by the United States, effective March 12, 2025, aims to protect domestic manufacturing but has significant implications for global trade dynamics, particularly concerning China's steel exports and the freight vessel industry. Impact on China's Steel Exports:
China, accounting for more than 50% of the world's steel production, has seen its exports surge, with projections indicating they will reach 109 million metric tons this year, an eight-year high. tariffs are expected to curb this growth.
